events.title: Farm to Craft: Grains in Global Folk Art
events.description: Folk art from Mingei's collection on the global cultural significance of grains like corn, wheat, rice, and quinoa.

events.title: Matrix Multiplied: Hybrid Approaches to Printmaking
events.description: Contemporary printmaking that blends and pushes beyond traditional techniques through layered, hybrid processes.

events.title: Cafés and Cabarets
events.venue: The San Diego Museum of Art
events.description: Belle Époque Paris in Toulouse-Lautrec lithographs and portraits from the Baldwin M. Baldwin collection, marking the museum's centennial.

events.title: Brothers in Arts: James Hubbell and Bert Hubbell
events.description: Parallel artistic lives of brothers James and Bert Hubbell, presented with the Ilan-Lael Foundation.

events.title: Giants: Art from the Dean Collection of Swizz Beatz and Alicia Keys
events.venue: Museum of Contemporary Art San Diego
events.description: Works by leading Black and global artists from the collection of Swizz Beatz and Alicia Keys.

events.title: David Adey: Sacrificial Bodies
events.description: First mid-career survey of San Diego artist David Adey, probing the body amid image culture and technology.

events.title: Kate Tova: A Place to Rest (My Tired Mind)
events.description: Immersive wildflower paintings confronting burnout, guilt, and the simple human need to rest.

events.title: To Catch a Fish
events.description: Over 130 works of art and craft exploring the bond between people and fish, plus contemporary pieces.

events.title: Prospect 2026
events.venue: Museum of Contemporary Art San Diego
events.description: A periodic survey of contemporary artists working across San Diego and the border region.

events.title: Clearly Indigenous
events.description: Contemporary Native American and Indigenous artists reframing craft, material, and identity.

events.title: Cecil Beaton: Fashion Photography Lecture
events.description: An evening lecture on Cecil Beaton's influential fashion photography, preceded by a 5 p.m. reception.
